Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentsAmendment by applicable with respect to 1980 and later model year automobiles, see , set out as an Effective Date note under .section 201(e) of Pub. L. 95–618section 201(g) of Pub. L. 95–618section 4064 of this title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–618, title II, § 231(g)92 Stat. 3189Nov. 9, 1978“(1)The amendments made by subsections (a) and (f) [amending this section and sections 4063 and 6412 of this title] shall apply with respect to articles sold after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Nov. 9, 1978“(2)For purposes of paragraph (1), an article shall not be considered sold on or before the date of the enactment of this Act [] unless possession or right to possession passes to the purchaser on or before such date.Nov. 9, 1978“(3)In the case of—“(A)a lease,“(B)a contract for the sale of an article providing that the price shall be paid by installments and title to the article sold does not pass until a future date notwithstanding partial payment by installments,“(C)a conditional sale, or“(D)a chattel mortgage arrangement providing that the sale price shall be paid in installments,entered into on or before the date of the enactment of this Act [], payments made after such date with respect to the article leased or sold shall, for purposes of this subsection, be considered as payments made with respect to an article sold after such date, if the lessor or vendor establishes that the amount of payments payable after such date with respect to such article has been reduced by an amount equal to that portion of the tax applicable with respect to the lease or sale of such article which is due and payable after such date. If the lessor or vendor does not establish that the payments have been so reduced, they shall be treated as payments made in respect of an article sold on or before the date of the enactment of this Act.”Nov. 9, 1978Amendment by effective on first day of first calendar month beginning more than 20 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 95–600section 701(ff)(3) of Pub. L. 95–600section 4221 of this titleNov. 6, 1978

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), section 4221 shall not apply with respect to the sale of any article unless the manufacturer, the first purchaser, and the second purchaser (if any) are all registered under this section. Registration under this section shall be made at such time, in such manner and form, and subject to such terms and conditions, as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe. A registration under this section may be used only in accordance with regulations prescribed under this section.

children.heading: Purchases by State and local governments
children.label: 1 Purchases by State and local governments
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to any State or local government in connection with the purchase by it of any article if such State or local government complies with such regulations relating to the use of exemption certificates in lieu of registration as the Secretary shall prescribe to carry out the purpose of this paragraph.

children.heading: Under regulations
children.label: 2 Under regulations
children.text: Subject to such regulations as the Secretary may prescribe for the purpose of this paragraph, the Secretary may relieve the purchaser or the second purchaser, or both, from the requirement of registering under this section.

children.heading: Certain purchases and sales by the United States
children.label: 3 Certain purchases and sales by the United States
children.text: Subsection (a) shall apply to purchases and sales by the United States only to the extent provided by regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Supplies for vessels or aircraft
children.label: 5 Supplies for vessels or aircraft
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to a sale of an article for use by the purchaser as supplies for any vessel or aircraft if such purchaser complies with such regulations relating to the use of exemption certificates in lieu of registration as the Secretary shall prescribe to carry out the purpose of this paragraph.

children.heading: Denial, revocation, or suspension of registration
children.label: c Denial, revocation, or suspension of registration

children.text: that such person has used such registration to avoid the payment of any tax imposed by this chapter, or to postpone or in any manner to interfere with the collection of any such tax, or
that such denial, revocation, or suspension is necessary to protect the revenue.

children.heading: Registration in the case of certain other exemptions
children.label: d Registration in the case of certain other exemptions
children.text: The provisions of this section may be extended to, and made applicable with respect to, the exemptions provided by sections 4053(6), 4064(b)(1)(C), 4101, and 4182(b), and the exemptions authorized under section 4293 in respect of the taxes imposed by this chapter, to the extent provided by regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: e Definitions
children.text: Terms used in this section which are defined in section 4221(d) shall have the meaning given to them by section 4221(d).
